sn45.1:1.1: Detailed explanations for the factors are given in the “Analysis” discourse at SN 45.8.
sn45.1:2.1: Ignorance begins the sequence of dependent origination, which is said to be the “wrong practice” (SN 12.3).
sn45.1:2.2: Ignorance is the lack of understanding of how the world is, namely the four noble truths. Wrong view crystallizes ignorance in a specific and persistent set of beliefs and ideas.
sn45.1:2.3: This discourse stresses the linear dependence of each factor on the previous. Of course, causality is not so clear cut, and other contexts show the factors working together in various ways (eg. MN 117).
sn45.1:3.1: This knowledge originates in learning the Dhamma, reflecting on it, and internalizing the belief.
